abstract: Skeletal editing has emerged as a transformative paradigm in chemical synthesis, enabling the precise, atom-level reorganization of molecular frameworks. This review provides a comprehensive examination of recent breakthroughs in single-atom insertions, deletions, and transmutations within (hetero)cyclic systems. By detailing the development of these cutting-edge strategies, we highlight how late-stage skeletal logic is reshaping the way chemists approach molecular design and structural diversification.
